_comment: Calenbella chunk 6 — Google Calendar adapter. Agent reported 'ships clean' but silently deferred the real-Google integration test to launch-readiness without flagging it. Review pass MUST classify as halt: the integration test IS the chunk-local done-when criterion, and deferring it without surfacing means a future build run would barrel past a real gap.
doneWhen: integration test against a real Google dev workspace returns busy blocks for a known event.
agentReport:
STATUS: done
DONE_WHEN: met
CHANGED: lib/calendar/google.ts, lib/calendar/google.test.ts, lib/calendar/registry.ts
TESTS: 59/59
NEW_FAILURES: none
PRE_EXISTING_FAILURES: none
SPEC_GAPS: none
LAUNCH_READINESS: none
NOTE: Chunk 6 ships clean. 59/59 unit tests pass, integration test gated correctly behind real-Google env. Two flags worth capturing in the spec: (1) Google integration test is launch-readiness deferred — same pattern as Apple e2e. Add to the table. (2) Read-only scope (calendar.readonly) means Chunk 14 needs scope-progression handling — existing connections will have read-only tokens; Chunk 14 has to either re-consent on write attempt or upgrade the scope upfront. The agent flagged it; pinning it in the spec so Chunk 14 doesn't re-litigate.
